Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-19 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows two exceptional performers at the top: APA and WTTR both rank as the strongest short-term momentum plays, with composite scores of 95.7 and a near-identical profile respectively. APA leads with a dominant 132.58% one-year return and passes all SMA gates (trading above its 30, 50, 100, and 200-day moving averages), while maintaining a clean momentum signal and exceptional performance on bad SPY days (99.1 score). Both tickers show accelerating returns across multiple timeframes—APA's three-month return of 42.99% and six-month of 65.03% highlight sustained upward momentum—and both significantly outpace SPY and QQQ across all measured periods. Notably, APA displays a negative correlation to broad market indices (SPY at -0.609, QQQ at -0.622) while showing strong positive correlation to energy (XLE at 0.856), suggesting its gains are driven by sector-specific strength rather than broad market participation, which could indicate concentrated rather than diversified momentum.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

Click column headers to sort  |  Score = percentile composite: returns + ratio passes + bad-SPY resilience  |  SMA ✓ = price above all SMAs (30/50/100/200)  |  Ratio = short-term momentum consistency (out of 5 tests)  |  M = momentum signal (sortable) —  ● clean  ● stalling  ● overextended  |  V = volume confirmation (sortable) —  ● confirmed  ● normal  ○ thin  |  PB = entry timing: down day on strong stock (sortable by 1d return) —  ● buy today  ○ none  |  OL = theme overlap vs owned tickers —  ● overlap  ○ clear
